title: Cora the Crane Truck and the Wobbly Sign
description: Cora learns how to carry a swinging woodland sign safely through an evening breeze.

Cora.role: Main character; a patient crane truck who solves lifting problems calmly
Cora.color: Lavender cab and rounded body, cream roof, coral boom tip, and teal side stripe
Cora.bodyShape: Compact six-wheeled crane truck with a short rounded hood, broad stable chassis, and one telescoping boom
Cora.face: Two large hazel windshield eyes with curved brows and a small friendly smile in the front grille
Cora.wheels: Six matching charcoal tires with teal hubs and cream fenders
Cora.accessories: Amber roof beacon, silver hook, two navy lifting straps, and a round moon emblem without text

Tuck.role: Supporting character; a careful little utility tractor
Tuck.appearance: Moss-green rounded tractor with tan roof, amber windshield eyes, four black tires, and a rear rope hitch

Pippa.role: Supporting character; the sign painter and delivery helper
Pippa.appearance: Coral compact pickup with cream bed rails, blue windshield eyes, four black tires, and paintbrush emblem without text

settingBible.Lantern Yard: A tidy hilltop work yard with a small cream shed, low stone wall, windsock, and potted marigolds
settingBible.Pinecone Reading Nook: A woodland clearing with rounded pines, a tiny open book shelter, smooth stones, lanterns, and a prepared sign base
settingBible.sign: A wide honey-brown wooden sign shaped like an acorn, painted only with a simple open-book picture and no readable words
settingBible.visualStyle: Bright polished 3D children's storybook illustration with rounded forms, soft cinematic light, and clean single-scene composition

continuityRules: Cora keeps the same lavender, cream, coral, and teal color blocking, six wheels, hazel eyes, amber beacon, silver hook, and moon emblem on every page.
Cora and all vehicles have no human-like arms or hands.
Tuck remains moss green with tan roof and rope hitch; Pippa remains coral with cream bed rails and blue eyes.
The acorn-shaped sign stays honey brown with one painted open-book picture and no readable text.
Two navy straps hold the sign whenever it hangs from Cora's hook; two pale guide ropes attach only on Pages 7-13.
The story moves from late afternoon through golden evening to a calm moonlit night.
Every raw illustration is a single full-bleed 4:3 scene with all required subjects above y=760 and only low-detail ground in the lower safe zone.
No raw artwork contains text, page numbers, badges, panels, logos, watermarks, collages, grids, or multiple scenes.

pages.text: One sunny afternoon, Cora the Crane Truck polished her silver hook at Lantern Yard. She was ready for a careful delivery.
pages.speechText: One sunny afternoon, Cora the Crane Truck polished her silver hook at Lantern Yard. She was ready for a careful delivery.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora waits in Lantern Yard beside the cream shed and marigolds, with her short boom folded and silver hook shining.
pages.continuityNotes: Establish Cora's lavender body, cream roof, coral boom tip, teal stripe and hubs, six tires, hazel eyes, beacon, hook, and moon emblem.

pages.text: Pippa arrived with a new wooden sign for the Pinecone Reading Nook. An open-book picture gleamed on its front.
pages.speechText: Pippa arrived with a new wooden sign for the Pinecone Reading Nook. An open-book picture gleamed on its front.
pages.illustrationScene: Pippa parks beside Cora with the acorn-shaped wooden sign secured flat in her pickup bed.
pages.continuityNotes: Introduce Pippa unchanged and show the honey-brown acorn sign with only a simple picture, not readable words.

pages.text: A playful hilltop breeze fluttered the marigolds. When Cora tested the lift, the wide sign grew wobbly.
pages.speechText: A playful hilltop breeze fluttered the marigolds. When Cora tested the lift, the wide sign grew wobbly.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora raises the strapped sign only a little above Pippa's bed as it tilts gently in the breeze and both vehicles watch closely.
pages.continuityNotes: The sign uses two navy straps and hangs low; show a safe gentle wobble with no people or hands.

pages.text: Cora lowered the sign at once. “A safe load should feel calm and steady,” she said.
pages.speechText: Cora lowered the sign at once. A safe load should feel calm and steady, she said.
pages.illustrationScene: The sign rests safely back in Pippa's bed while Cora studies the windsock and keeps her boom low.
pages.continuityNotes: Sign is supported, straps remain attached, and Cora's expression is thoughtful rather than worried.

pages.text: Cora shortened her boom and turned her truck toward the breeze. The next tiny lift wobbled much less.
pages.speechText: Cora shortened her boom and turned her truck toward the breeze. The next tiny lift wobbled much less.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora faces the windsock with her boom short and centered while the sign hangs just above the pickup bed, nearly level.
pages.continuityNotes: Keep boom compact and sign low; maintain the exact two navy straps and unchanged sign face.

pages.text: Tuck rolled over with two soft guide ropes on his hitch. “These can help the sign stay straight,” he said.
pages.speechText: Tuck rolled over with two soft guide ropes on his hitch. These can help the sign stay straight, he said.
pages.illustrationScene: Tuck joins Cora and Pippa in the yard, carrying two neatly coiled pale ropes on his rear hitch beside the lowered sign.
pages.continuityNotes: Introduce Tuck's moss-green body, tan roof, amber eyes, four tires, and rear hitch. Ropes are still coiled.

pages.text: Pippa clipped one guide rope to each lower corner. Tuck held both ropes gently with his sturdy hitch.
pages.speechText: Pippa clipped one guide rope to each lower corner. Tuck held both ropes gently with his sturdy hitch.
pages.illustrationScene: The sign hangs low from Cora's two navy straps while pale guide ropes run from its lower corners to Tuck's hitch and Pippa checks from beside her truck.
pages.continuityNotes: Show attachment results without human-like hands; the guide ropes are pale and gently taut, and the sign remains above the safe zone.

pages.text: Cora lifted again, slow and smooth. The sign stayed straight while Tuck rolled one small step beside it.
pages.speechText: Cora lifted again, slow and smooth. The sign stayed straight while Tuck rolled one small step beside it.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora performs a stable practice lift in the yard as Tuck moves beside the level sign and Pippa smiles near the shed.
pages.continuityNotes: Sign is level at a low transport height with two straps and two ropes; all three vehicles retain locked designs.

pages.text: Together they left Lantern Yard along the smooth pine path. Cora carried the steady sign low and close.
pages.speechText: Together they left Lantern Yard along the smooth pine path. Cora carried the steady sign low and close.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora leads onto a pine-lined path carrying the low sign, Tuck guides beside it, and Pippa follows as the yard gate recedes behind.
pages.continuityNotes: Bridge to Page 10 with the same pine path, low sign height, straps, ropes, travel order, and warm light.

pages.text: At the first bend, the same breeze slipped between the pine trees. Cora stopped before the sign could sway.
pages.speechText: At the first bend, the same breeze slipped between the pine trees. Cora stopped before the sign could sway.
pages.illustrationScene: At the next bend of the same pine path, Cora pauses with the sign level while Tuck and Pippa stop in their established positions.
pages.continuityNotes: Continue Page 9 geography and formation exactly; the sign has not begun swinging and remains safely low.

pages.text: A thick hedge made a quiet windbreak beside the path. The friends waited there until the pine needles grew still.
pages.speechText: A thick hedge made a quiet windbreak beside the path. The friends waited there until the pine needles grew still.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora, Tuck, and Pippa wait calmly behind a rounded green hedge, with the suspended sign sheltered and level.
pages.continuityNotes: Keep all rigging attached and show calm waiting; the hedge blocks wind without hiding required vehicles or sign.

pages.text: Soon the lanterns of the reading nook twinkled ahead. Cora carried the sign through the last quiet turn.
pages.speechText: Soon the lanterns of the reading nook twinkled ahead. Cora carried the sign through the last quiet turn.
pages.illustrationScene: The three vehicles approach the Pinecone Reading Nook, where warm lanterns, the tiny book shelter, and prepared sign base appear ahead.
pages.continuityNotes: Reading nook matches setting bible; sign is still suspended low with straps and ropes as they approach the empty base.

pages.text: Cora lowered the sign into its prepared base. Tuck kept the guide ropes steady until it stood safely on its own.
pages.speechText: Cora lowered the sign into its prepared base. Tuck kept the guide ropes steady until it stood safely on its own.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora gently seats the acorn sign in the prepared woodland base while Tuck's ropes keep it aligned and Pippa watches nearby.
pages.continuityNotes: Show final lowering rather than repair work; straps and guide ropes remain attached until the sign is seated.

pages.text: The new sign stood straight beside the cozy books. “Slow, low, and steady worked,” Cora said with a proud smile.
pages.speechText: The new sign stood straight beside the cozy books. Slow, low, and steady worked, Cora said with a proud smile.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora, Pippa, and Tuck admire the installed acorn sign beside the glowing book shelter as the last sunset colors fill the clearing.
pages.continuityNotes: Hook is empty, straps and ropes are neatly coiled on Tuck's hitch, and the sign stands independently with no readable words.

pages.text: That night, Cora folded her boom beneath the quiet moon. She closed her sleepy hazel eyes and dreamed of steady, gentle lifts.
pages.speechText: That night, Cora folded her boom beneath the quiet moon. She closed her sleepy hazel eyes and dreamed of steady, gentle lifts.
pages.illustrationScene: Cora rests back at Lantern Yard under a crescent moon, boom folded, eyes closed, with the shed window glowing softly.
pages.continuityNotes: Return to Page 1 yard; Cora has no sign or ropes, retains every locked feature, and ends in a calm bedtime pose.
